---
title: End-to-End Encryption Explained
author: SecNotes
date: 2024-02-27
description: E2E encryption ensures only communicating parties can read messages
tags: ["#encryption", "#privacy"]
---
E2E encryption ensures only communicating parties can read messages. No intermediary, not even the service provider, can decrypt the content.

## How It Works

The Noise Protocol framework provides a clean way to establish encrypted channels between peers. Combined with blind pairing, you get privacy-preserving authentication.
